instruction: Show C++ configuration patterns using TOML parsing with compile-time defaults and runtime overrides.
output:
<think>
C++ config management differs from managed languages because there's no reflection. Common approaches:
1. toml++ or toml11 for TOML parsing
2. nlohmann/json for JSON configs
3. Compile-time defaults via constexpr
4. Runtime overrides from files and env vars
5. Strong typing through config structs

I'll use toml++ which is header-only and handles TOML 1.0. The pattern: define config struct with defaults, load from file, override from env vars, validate.
</think>

```cpp
#include <toml++/toml.hpp>
#include <iostream>
#include <string>
#include <optional>
#include <cstdlib>
#include <stdexcept>
#include <vector>

struct ServerConfig {
    std::string host = "0.0.0.0";
    uint16_t port = 8080;
    int workers = 4;
    size_t max_body_size = 10 * 1024 * 1024;
};

struct DatabaseConfig {
    std::string host = "localhost";
    uint16_t port = 5432;
    std::string name = "myapp";
    std::string user = "postgres";
    std::string password;
    int pool_max = 20;
    bool ssl = false;

    std::string dsn() const {
        return "postgresql://" + user + ":" + password + "@" +
               host + ":" + std::to_string(port) + "/" + name;
    }
};

struct AppConfig {
    std::string app_name = "MyApp";
    std::string environment = "development";
    ServerConfig server;
    DatabaseConfig database;
    std::string log_level = "info";

    static AppConfig load(const std::string& path) {
        AppConfig cfg;
        try {
            auto tbl = toml::parse_file(path);
            cfg.app_name = tbl["app_name"].value_or(cfg.app_name);
            cfg.environment = tbl["environment"].value_or(cfg.environment);
            cfg.log_level = tbl["log_level"].value_or(cfg.log_level);

            if (auto srv = tbl["server"].as_table()) {
                cfg.server.host = (*srv)["host"].value_or(cfg.server.host);
                cfg.server.port = static_cast<uint16_t>(
                    (*srv)["port"].value_or(static_cast<int64_t>(cfg.server.port)));
                cfg.server.workers = static_cast<int>(
                    (*srv)["workers"].value_or(static_cast<int64_t>(cfg.server.workers)));
            }

            if (auto db = tbl["database"].as_table()) {
                cfg.database.host = (*db)["host"].value_or(cfg.database.host);
                cfg.database.port = static_cast<uint16_t>(
                    (*db)["port"].value_or(static_cast<int64_t>(cfg.database.port)));
                cfg.database.name = (*db)["name"].value_or(cfg.database.name);
                cfg.database.user = (*db)["user"].value_or(cfg.database.user);
                cfg.database.password = (*db)["password"].value_or(std::string{});
                cfg.database.pool_max = static_cast<int>(
                    (*db)["pool_max"].value_or(static_cast<int64_t>(cfg.database.pool_max)));
                cfg.database.ssl = (*db)["ssl"].value_or(cfg.database.ssl);
            }
        } catch (const toml::parse_error& e) {
            throw std::runtime_error(std::string("Config parse error: ") + e.what());
        }

        cfg.apply_env_overrides();
        cfg.validate();
        return cfg;
    }

    void apply_env_overrides() {
        if (auto* v = std::getenv("APP_PORT"))
            server.port = static_cast<uint16_t>(std::stoi(v));
        if (auto* v = std::getenv("APP_WORKERS"))
            server.workers = std::stoi(v);
        if (auto* v = std::getenv("DB_HOST"))
            database.host = v;
        if (auto* v = std::getenv("DB_PASSWORD"))
            database.password = v;
        if (auto* v = std::getenv("LOG_LEVEL"))
            log_level = v;
    }

    void validate() const {
        std::vector<std::string> errors;
        if (server.port == 0)
            errors.push_back("server.port cannot be 0");
        if (server.workers < 1)
            errors.push_back("server.workers must be >= 1");
        if (database.pool_max < 1)
            errors.push_back("database.pool_max must be >= 1");
        if (environment == "production" && database.password.empty())
            errors.push_back("database.password required in production");
        if (!errors.empty()) {
            std::string msg = "Config validation failed:\n";
            for (const auto& e : errors) msg += "  - " + e + "\n";
            throw std::runtime_error(msg);
        }
    }
};

int main() {
    try {
        auto cfg = AppConfig::load("config.toml");
        std::cout << cfg.app_name << " (" << cfg.environment << ")\n";
        std::cout << "Server: " << cfg.server.host << ":" << cfg.server.port << "\n";
        std::cout << "DB: " << cfg.database.dsn() << "\n";
    } catch (const std::exception& e) {
        std::cerr << "Error: " << e.what() << "\n";
        return 1;
    }
}
```

instruction: Show compile-time configuration in C++ using constexpr, templates, and build-system integration for zero-runtime-cost config.
output:
<think>
Compile-time config in C++ uses constexpr for values known at build time. Benefits: zero runtime cost, optimized away by compiler, type-safe. Use cases: buffer sizes, feature flags, platform-specific values, protocol constants.

Patterns: constexpr variables, if constexpr for conditional compilation, template specialization for platform-specific behavior, build-system generated headers.
</think>

```cpp
#include <cstddef>
#include <cstdint>
#include <string_view>
#include <iostream>
#include <array>

// === Compile-time config via constexpr ===

namespace config {

// Build mode (set by CMake: -DBUILD_MODE=release)
#ifdef NDEBUG
    constexpr bool is_debug = false;
    constexpr std::string_view build_mode = "release";
#else
    constexpr bool is_debug = true;
    constexpr std::string_view build_mode = "debug";
#endif

// Version (injected by build system via -DAPP_VERSION="1.2.3")
#ifndef APP_VERSION
    #define APP_VERSION "0.0.0-dev"
#endif
constexpr std::string_view version = APP_VERSION;

// Buffer sizes — compile-time, zero overhead
constexpr size_t read_buffer_size = is_debug ? 4096 : 65536;
constexpr size_t write_buffer_size = is_debug ? 4096 : 65536;
constexpr size_t max_connections = is_debug ? 10 : 10000;
constexpr size_t thread_pool_size = is_debug ? 2 : 8;

// Protocol constants
constexpr uint16_t default_port = 8080;
constexpr uint32_t max_message_size = 16 * 1024 * 1024; // 16MB
constexpr auto request_timeout_ms = 30'000;

// Feature flags (compile-time)
constexpr bool enable_tls = true;
constexpr bool enable_compression = true;
constexpr bool enable_metrics = !is_debug;  // Metrics only in release

// Platform detection
#if defined(_WIN32)
    constexpr std::string_view platform = "windows";
#elif defined(__linux__)
    constexpr std::string_view platform = "linux";
#elif defined(__APPLE__)
    constexpr std::string_view platform = "macos";
#else
    constexpr std::string_view platform = "unknown";
#endif

} // namespace config

// === Using if constexpr for zero-cost branching ===

template<typename T>
void log_message(std::string_view level, const T& msg) {
    if constexpr (config::is_debug) {
        // Debug build: detailed logging with file/line
        std::cout << "[" << level << "] " << msg << "\n";
    } else {
        // Release build: only warn and above
        if (level == "WARN" || level == "ERROR") {
            std::cout << "[" << level << "] " << msg << "\n";
        }
        // Other levels compiled away entirely
    }
}

// === Compile-time computed lookup tables ===

constexpr auto make_hex_table() {
    std::array<char, 256> table{};
    for (int i = 0; i < 256; ++i) {
        if (i >= '0' && i <= '9') table[i] = static_cast<char>(i - '0');
        else if (i >= 'a' && i <= 'f') table[i] = static_cast<char>(i - 'a' + 10);
        else if (i >= 'A' && i <= 'F') table[i] = static_cast<char>(i - 'A' + 10);
        else table[i] = -1;
    }
    return table;
}

constexpr auto hex_table = make_hex_table();

// === Static assertions for config consistency ===

static_assert(config::read_buffer_size >= 4096, "Read buffer too small");
static_assert(config::max_connections >= 1, "Must allow at least 1 connection");
static_assert(config::thread_pool_size >= 1, "Thread pool needs at least 1 thread");
static_assert(config::max_message_size <= 64 * 1024 * 1024, "Message size too large");

int main() {
    std::cout << "App " << config::version
              << " (" << config::build_mode << ", " << config::platform << ")\n";
    std::cout << "Buffer: " << config::read_buffer_size << " bytes\n";
    std::cout << "Max connections: " << config::max_connections << "\n";
    std::cout << "TLS: " << (config::enable_tls ? "yes" : "no") << "\n";

    log_message("DEBUG", "This only appears in debug builds");
    log_message("ERROR", "This always appears");

    // hex_table is computed at compile time — zero runtime cost
    std::cout << "Hex 'a' = " << static_cast<int>(hex_table['a']) << "\n";
}
```

instruction: Show C++ patterns for runtime configuration with change notification, thread-safe access, and configuration inheritance.
output:
<think>
Runtime config in C++ that's thread-safe and supports change notification requires:
1. Atomic access or mutex for thread safety
2. Observer pattern for change notification
3. Immutable config objects swapped atomically (copy-on-write)
4. Configuration inheritance: base config -> environment config -> overrides

The best pattern: store config as shared_ptr<const Config> and swap atomically. Readers get the shared_ptr (cheap), writers create a new Config and swap.
</think>

```cpp
#include <atomic>
#include <functional>
#include <iostream>
#include <memory>
#include <mutex>
#include <string>
#include <unordered_map>
#include <vector>

struct Config {
    std::string log_level = "info";
    int rate_limit = 100;
    bool maintenance_mode = false;
    std::unordered_map<std::string, bool> feature_flags;

    Config with_log_level(std::string level) const {
        Config copy = *this;
        copy.log_level = std::move(level);
        return copy;
    }

    Config with_rate_limit(int limit) const {
        Config copy = *this;
        copy.rate_limit = limit;
        return copy;
    }

    Config with_feature(const std::string& name, bool enabled) const {
        Config copy = *this;
        copy.feature_flags[name] = enabled;
        return copy;
    }
};

class ConfigManager {
    using Listener = std::function<void(const Config&, const Config&)>;

    std::shared_ptr<const Config> current_;
    mutable std::mutex mu_;
    std::vector<Listener> listeners_;

public:
    explicit ConfigManager(Config initial)
        : current_(std::make_shared<const Config>(std::move(initial))) {}

    // Thread-safe read — returns shared_ptr (cheap copy)
    std::shared_ptr<const Config> get() const {
        std::lock_guard lock(mu_);
        return current_;
    }

    // Thread-safe update — atomic swap
    void update(std::function<Config(const Config&)> modifier) {
        std::lock_guard lock(mu_);
        auto old = current_;
        auto new_cfg = std::make_shared<const Config>(modifier(*old));
        current_ = new_cfg;

        for (const auto& listener : listeners_) {
            listener(*old, *new_cfg);
        }
    }

    void on_change(Listener listener) {
        std::lock_guard lock(mu_);
        listeners_.push_back(std::move(listener));
    }
};

// Config inheritance: base -> env -> overrides
Config build_config(const std::string& env) {
    // Base defaults
    Config base;
    base.log_level = "info";
    base.rate_limit = 100;
    base.feature_flags = {{"cache", false}, {"metrics", false}};

    // Environment overrides
    if (env == "production") {
        base.log_level = "warn";
        base.rate_limit = 1000;
        base.feature_flags["cache"] = true;
        base.feature_flags["metrics"] = true;
    } else if (env == "staging") {
        base.rate_limit = 500;
        base.feature_flags["cache"] = true;
    }

    return base;
}

int main() {
    auto initial = build_config("production");
    ConfigManager mgr(initial);

    mgr.on_change([](const Config& old_cfg, const Config& new_cfg) {
        if (old_cfg.log_level != new_cfg.log_level) {
            std::cout << "Log level changed: " << old_cfg.log_level
                      << " -> " << new_cfg.log_level << "\n";
        }
        if (old_cfg.maintenance_mode != new_cfg.maintenance_mode) {
            std::cout << "Maintenance mode: " << new_cfg.maintenance_mode << "\n";
        }
    });

    // Read config (thread-safe, lock-free after acquiring shared_ptr)
    auto cfg = mgr.get();
    std::cout << "Rate limit: " << cfg->rate_limit << "\n";

    // Update config (thread-safe)
    mgr.update([](const Config& c) {
        return c.with_log_level("debug").with_rate_limit(50);
    });

    // Toggle maintenance mode
    mgr.update([](const Config& c) {
        Config copy = c;
        copy.maintenance_mode = true;
        return copy;
    });
}
```

instruction: Show a C++ YAML configuration loader with error reporting that pinpoints the exact line of config errors.
output:
<think>
YAML config in C++ is handled well by yaml-cpp. The key challenge is error reporting — when a config value is wrong, the user needs to know WHICH line in which file.

yaml-cpp provides line/column info through YAML::Mark on each node. I'll build a loader that catches parse errors AND validation errors with line numbers.
</think>

```cpp
#include <yaml-cpp/yaml.h>
#include <iostream>
#include <string>
#include <vector>
#include <stdexcept>

struct ConfigError {
    std::string field;
    std::string message;
    int line = -1;
    int column = -1;

    std::string format() const {
        std::string loc;
        if (line >= 0) loc = " (line " + std::to_string(line + 1) + ")";
        return "  " + field + loc + ": " + message;
    }
};

class ConfigValidator {
    std::vector<ConfigError> errors_;

public:
    template<typename T>
    T require(const YAML::Node& parent, const std::string& key, const std::string& path) {
        auto node = parent[key];
        if (!node.IsDefined()) {
            errors_.push_back({path + "." + key, "required but missing"});
            return T{};
        }
        try {
            return node.as<T>();
        } catch (const YAML::Exception& e) {
            auto mark = node.Mark();
            errors_.push_back({
                path + "." + key,
                "invalid value: " + std::string(e.what()),
                mark.line, mark.column
            });
            return T{};
        }
    }

    template<typename T>
    T optional(const YAML::Node& parent, const std::string& key, T default_val) {
        auto node = parent[key];
        if (!node.IsDefined()) return default_val;
        try {
            return node.as<T>();
        } catch (...) {
            return default_val;
        }
    }

    void check(bool condition, const std::string& field, const std::string& msg, int line = -1) {
        if (!condition) errors_.push_back({field, msg, line});
    }

    bool has_errors() const { return !errors_.empty(); }

    void throw_if_errors() const {
        if (errors_.empty()) return;
        std::string msg = "Config validation failed:\n";
        for (const auto& e : errors_) msg += e.format() + "\n";
        throw std::runtime_error(msg);
    }
};

struct AppConfig {
    std::string name;
    std::string environment;
    struct {
        std::string host;
        int port;
        int workers;
    } server;
    struct {
        std::string host;
        int port;
        std::string name;
        std::string user;
        std::string password;
    } database;

    static AppConfig load(const std::string& path) {
        YAML::Node root;
        try {
            root = YAML::LoadFile(path);
        } catch (const YAML::ParserException& e) {
            throw std::runtime_error(
                "YAML parse error in " + path + ":\n  " + e.what()
            );
        } catch (const YAML::BadFile&) {
            throw std::runtime_error("Cannot open config file: " + path);
        }

        ConfigValidator v;
        AppConfig cfg;

        cfg.name = v.optional<std::string>(root, "name", "MyApp");
        cfg.environment = v.require<std::string>(root, "environment", "root");

        if (auto srv = root["server"]) {
            cfg.server.host = v.optional<std::string>(srv, "host", "0.0.0.0");
            cfg.server.port = v.require<int>(srv, "port", "server");
            cfg.server.workers = v.optional<int>(srv, "workers", 4);

            v.check(cfg.server.port > 0 && cfg.server.port <= 65535,
                    "server.port", "must be 1-65535",
                    srv["port"].Mark().line);
            v.check(cfg.server.workers >= 1,
                    "server.workers", "must be >= 1");
        } else {
            v.check(false, "server", "section is required");
        }

        if (auto db = root["database"]) {
            cfg.database.host = v.require<std::string>(db, "host", "database");
            cfg.database.port = v.optional<int>(db, "port", 5432);
            cfg.database.name = v.require<std::string>(db, "name", "database");
            cfg.database.user = v.require<std::string>(db, "user", "database");
            cfg.database.password = v.optional<std::string>(db, "password", "");
        } else {
            v.check(false, "database", "section is required");
        }

        v.throw_if_errors();
        return cfg;
    }
};

int main() {
    try {
        auto cfg = AppConfig::load("config.yaml");
        std::cout << cfg.name << " (" << cfg.environment << ")\n";
        std::cout << "Server: " << cfg.server.host << ":" << cfg.server.port << "\n";
    } catch (const std::exception& e) {
        std::cerr << e.what() << "\n";
        return 1;
    }
}
```
